pattern: For each end position, the earliest start that still covers all three letters fixes how many valid substrings end there, so a sliding window summed across ends counts them all in linear time.
hints: Fix the right end of a substring. Once it covers all three letters, extending the left end further right keeps it valid until you drop a required letter.
Track the last index at which you saw each of the three letters; the smallest of those three is the latest start that still includes every letter.

Walk the string with a right pointer, updating the last-seen index of a, b, c. After each step, add (1 + min(last_a, last_b, last_c)) when all three have been seen — that is the count of valid substrings ending here.

edge_inputs.description: long run of one letter before the others arrive
missing one letter entirely, zero substrings
all three letters but only at the extremes
trailing tail after the first complete window

stress.description: long random string over the three-letter alphabet
stress.note: dense coverage of all three letters keeps most ends valid
